news.title: Argentina vs. England: A semifinal about more than football
news.lede: This combination of file photos created on July 12, 2026, shows Argentina's forward #10 Lionel Messi in Arlington on June 27, 2026 (L),ja and England's forward #09 Harry Kane in Arlington on June 17, 2026. England and Argentina will meet in a 2026 Wo. ATLANTA - A fierce and deeply rooted football rivalry will reignite in Atlanta on Wednesday as Argentina and England face off at Atlanta Stadium with a spot in the World Cup final on the line. Argentina advanced to the semifinal round by defeating Switzerland 3-1 in extra time, a victory that followed hard-fought wins against Egypt and Cabo Verde earlier in the tournament.

news.title: How to Watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup Semifinal Live Without Cable (and Even for Free)
news.lede: And then there were four. We've officially entered the 2026 World Cup semifinals, and we'll see two nail-biting matches this week to decide who will compete in the final on Sunday. What started as a massive worldwide competition between 48 countries has been narrowed down to France, Spain, England, and Argentina. Today's semifinal game is between heavyweights, France and Spain .

news.title: France at the World Cup 2026: Squad, fixtures, group and history
news.lede: Everything you need to know about France at the 2026 World Cup: their fixtures, squad, qualifying record, key players, coach and tournament history. France were runners-up last time and will have an eye on the gold medals at FIFA World Cup 2026. Didier Deschamps' side, led by captain Kylian Mbappé, topped Group I ahead of Norway, Senegal and Iraq to reach the knockout phase. They then beat Sweden in the round of 32, Paraguay in the last 16 and Morocco in the quarter-finals.

news.title: ‘For Diego’: spectre of Maradona looms over Argentina ahead of England clash
news.lede: The image of the national icon has been ever present through this World Cup, in banners and songs and memory. It adds a fresh layer to Wednesday’s semi-final. Not two minutes after his side’s dramatic, extra-time quarter-final victory over Switzerland on Saturday, the Argentina head coach, Lionel Scaloni, was already getting asked about the semi-final. Looming on the horizon was a match against bitter rivals England.

news.title: World Cup predictions: Expert picks for Argentina-England, Spain-France
news.lede: The 2026 World Cup semifinals feature two matches soccer fans dreamed about before the tournament began. Spain and and Argentina vs. England pits four powerhouses against one another, with the game's biggest stars on the field together to decide the winner of soccer's most important trophy. The final step before the World Cup ends, begins on Tuesday, July 14 when France and Spain meet in Arlington, Texas, with a spotlight on France's Kylian Mbappe and Spain's Lamine Yamal as they renew their La Liga rivalry on a grand stage.

news.title: How Oasis’ ‘Wonderwall’ Became the Unofficial Anthem for England’s Epic World Cup Run
news.lede: The England men’s football team didn’t commission an official anthem for this year’s FIFA World Cup, but perhaps the answer was staring them in the face all along. After every match in the Three Lions’ current unbeaten run in the tournament, the team have lined up in front of their traveling supporters and been serenaded with one of the most popular rock songs in history: Oasis’ 1995 classic “Wonderwall.” The song has since experienced a remarkable uptick in streams and sales; at the time of writing, the song is up to No. 2 on the Spotify’s top 50 global chart, trailing only Shakira and Burna Boy’s “Dai Dai,” an official anthem for the tournament. It has leapt 20 places week-on-week on the U.K.’s Official Singles Chart and could go even further and best its previous best of No.

news.title: World Cup semifinals start Tuesday, July 14. What you need to know
news.lede: The FIFA Men's World Cup is down to four semifinalists, each nation grasping to reach the final that crowns soccer's world champion on Sunday, July 19. France and Spain battle it out on Tuesday, July 14, starting at 3 p.m. England and Argentina go at it beginning at 3 p.m., Wednesday, July 15. Everything you need to know: FIFA Men's World Cup 2026 coverage from USA TODAY.
